name: Atzem Pilafi
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 pounds Tomatoes, peeled and strained OR
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash and dry the meat.
2. Brown 2/3 of the butter in a large pot over medium heat.
3. Add meat and brown on all sides. Add onions and continue cooking until they become a light golden color.
4. Add tomatoes or diluted tomato paste, salt, pepper, and water. Stir to combine.
5. Cover the pot and simmer until the meat is tender (about 1 hour).
6. Transfer the meat to a casserole dish and keep it warm.
7. Strain the sauce; measure it. If necessary, add more water to make 8 or 9 cups.
8. Pour the strained sauce into the large pot and bring to a boil.
9. Add rice. Stir at the start to prevent sticking.
10. Cover and simmer until most of the liquid is absorbed (20 to 30 minutes).
11. Remove from heat.
12. Add meat and mix well.
13. Brown the remaining butter and pour it over the rice.
14. Cover the pot with a clean towel, then cover the towel with the pot lid.
15. Let stand for 5 minutes.
16. Serve hot. Serves 6 to 10.
